'''Jack Hughes''' (born 15 November 2000) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} for the [[London Broncos]] in the [[Super League]].

He previously played for the [[London Skolars]] in [[2023 RFL League 1|League 1]], [[rugby sevens]] for the [[Northampton Saints]] and [[rugby union]] as a [[Rugby union positions#Hooker|hooker]] for the [[Bedford Blues]] in the [[RFU Championship]]. He also represented England U18s at age grade level.

==Early life and education==
He was born in [[London]], England. He is the son of London Broncos former owner David Hughes. He was educated at [[Uppingham School]].<ref name="uppingham">{{cite web|url=https://www.uppingham.co.uk/news/2019-03-07/northampton-saints-senior-academy-success|title=Northampton Saints Senior Academy Success {{pipe}} News {{pipe}} Uppingham School|website=uppingham.co.uk|access-date=2024-03-24}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Rugby Union===
Hughes came through the [[Northampton Saints]] academy and featured numerous items for their [[Rugby sevens|Sevens team]].<ref name="zeeon">{{cite web|url=https://www.zeeon.co.uk/away-player/tommy-matthews|author=London Scottish|title=Jack Hughes|website=Zeeon|access-date=2024-03-24}}</ref>

He spent time on loan from [[Northampton Saints|Northampton]] at the [[Bedford Blues]] in 2021. He later signed on permanent deal at the [[Goldington Road]] club.<ref name="bedfordrugby">{{cite web|url=https://bedfordrugby.co.uk/news/2022/05/jack-hughes-agrees-stay-2223|website=bedfordrugby.co.uk|title=Jack Hughes agrees to stay in 22/23 : Bedford Blues RFC|access-date=2024-03-24}}</ref>

===Rugby League===
Hughes joined the [[London Skolars]] on a short-term deal at the end of the [[2022–23 RFU Championship]] season.

He joined the [[London Broncos]] first team squad at the start of the [[2024 Super League season|2024 season]].

He made his professional debut for the London Broncos in 2024 against the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[2024 Challenge Cup|Challenge Cup]].<ref name="co.uk">{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68616850|title=Challenge Cup: Warrington Wolves 42-0 London Broncos - Matty Ashton treble helps Wire reach last eight - BBC Sport|website=bbc.co.uk|date=23 March 2024 |access-date=2024-03-24}}</ref><ref name="warringtonguardian">{{cite web|url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/wolves/reports/24206546.warrington-wolves-42-london-broncos-0-challenge-cup/|title=Warrington Wolves 42 London Broncos 0, Challenge Cup|website=Warrington Guardian|access-date=2024-03-24}}</ref>
